Fungal infection.description: A fungal infection is caused by fungi — microscopic organisms that can live on skin, hair, nails, and mucous membranes. They are generally not life-threatening but can cause significant discomfort.
Fungal infection.causes: Direct contact with infected person or animal, sharing personal items like towels or combs, walking barefoot in moist public areas, weakened immune system.
Fungal infection.symptoms: Itching, skin rashes, redness, scaling, cracking of skin, nodal skin eruptions, watering from eyes, dischromic patches.
Fungal infection.prevention: Keep skin clean and dry, avoid sharing personal items, wear breathable fabrics, maintain good hygiene, treat infections promptly.

Allergy.description: An allergy is an immune system response to a foreign substance that is not typically harmful to the body. These foreign substances are called allergens and can include pollen, bee venom, or pet dander.
Allergy.causes: Exposure to allergens such as pollen, dust mites, animal dander, certain foods, insect stings, medications, or latex.
Allergy.symptoms: Continuous sneezing, shivering, chills, watering from eyes, skin rash, itching, runny nose, shortness of breath.
Allergy.prevention: Identify and avoid known allergens, use air purifiers, keep windows closed during high pollen season, use hypoallergenic products.

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a chronic digestive disorder where stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus, irritating its lining.
GERD.causes: Hiatal hernia, obesity, pregnancy, smoking, certain medications, eating large meals or lying down after meals.
GERD.symptoms: Stomach pain, acidity, ulcers on tongue, vomiting, cough, heartburn, regurgitation, difficulty swallowing.
GERD.prevention: Maintain healthy weight, eat smaller meals, avoid trigger foods, do not lie down after eating, elevate head of bed.

Chronic cholestasis.description: Chronic cholestasis is a liver condition where bile cannot flow from the liver to the duodenum, causing a buildup of bile acids in the liver and blood.
Chronic cholestasis.causes: Primary biliary cholangitis, primary sclerosing cholangitis, intrahepatic cholestasis, medications, infections.
Chronic cholestasis.symptoms: Itching, vomiting, yellowish skin, nausea, loss of appetite, dark urine, pale stools, fatigue.
Chronic cholestasis.prevention: Avoid medications that can cause liver damage, limit alcohol consumption, maintain healthy weight, regular liver function tests.

Drug Reaction.description: A drug reaction is an adverse response to a medication that can range from mild to severe. It can occur with prescription drugs, over-the-counter medications, or supplements.
Drug Reaction.causes: Allergic response to medication, medication overdose, drug interactions, genetic factors affecting drug metabolism.
Drug Reaction.symptoms: Itching, skin rash, stomach pain, burning micturition, spotting urination, hives, swelling, difficulty breathing.
Drug Reaction.prevention: Inform doctors of all medications and allergies, follow prescribed dosages, avoid known allergens, carry medical alert information.

Peptic ulcer disease.description: Peptic ulcer disease involves open sores that develop on the inner lining of the stomach and the upper portion of the small intestine.
Peptic ulcer disease.causes: H. pylori bacterial infection, regular use of NSAIDs, excessive alcohol consumption, smoking, stress.
Peptic ulcer disease.symptoms: Vomiting, indigestion, loss of appetite, abdominal pain, passage of gases, burning stomach pain, nausea.
Peptic ulcer disease.prevention: Avoid NSAIDs, limit alcohol, quit smoking, manage stress, eat regular meals, avoid spicy foods.

AIDS.description: AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome) is the most severe phase of HIV infection. People with AIDS have such badly damaged immune systems that they get an increasing number of severe illnesses.
AIDS.causes: Infection with HIV virus through unprotected sexual contact, sharing needles, blood transfusions, mother to child during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
AIDS.symptoms: Muscle wasting, patches in throat, high fever, fatigue, extra-marital contacts history, rapid weight loss, recurring infections.
AIDS.prevention: Practice safe sex, use clean needles, get tested regularly, take antiretroviral therapy if HIV positive, avoid blood-to-blood contact.

Diabetes.description: Diabetes is a chronic disease that occurs when the pancreas does not produce enough insulin or when the body cannot effectively use the insulin it produces.
Diabetes.causes: Genetic factors, obesity, sedentary lifestyle, poor diet, autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing cells.
Diabetes.symptoms: Fatigue, weight loss, restlessness, lethargy, irregular sugar level, increased thirst, frequent urination, blurred vision.
Diabetes.prevention: Maintain healthy weight, exercise regularly, eat balanced diet, avoid sugary foods, regular blood sugar monitoring.

Gastroenteritis.description: Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically resulting from bacterial toxins, viral infection, or contaminated food or water.
Gastroenteritis.causes: Viral infections (norovirus, rotavirus), bacterial contamination, food poisoning, parasitic infections.
Gastroenteritis.symptoms: Vomiting, sunken eyes, dehydration, diarrhoea, stomach pain, nausea, fever, muscle aches.
Gastroenteritis.prevention: Wash hands frequently, eat properly cooked food, drink clean water, avoid close contact with infected persons.

Bronchial Asthma.description: Bronchial asthma is a long-term inflammatory disease of the airways that makes breathing difficult. It causes recurring periods of wheezing, chest tightness, shortness of breath.
Bronchial Asthma.causes: Allergens, air pollutants, respiratory infections, physical activity, cold air, medications like aspirin.
Bronchial Asthma.symptoms: Fatigue, cough, high fever, breathlessness, family history, wheezing, chest tightness, shortness of breath.
Bronchial Asthma.prevention: Identify and avoid triggers, use prescribed inhalers, maintain healthy weight, exercise regularly, avoid smoking.

Hypertension.description: Hypertension (high blood pressure) is a condition in which the force of blood against artery walls is consistently too high, potentially causing health problems like heart disease.
Hypertension.causes: Age, family history, obesity, lack of physical activity, excess salt intake, alcohol, smoking, stress.
Hypertension.symptoms: Headache, chest pain, dizziness, loss of balance, lack of concentration, nosebleed, irregular heartbeat.
Hypertension.prevention: Reduce sodium intake, exercise regularly, maintain healthy weight, limit alcohol, stop smoking, manage stress.

Migraine.description: A migraine is a neurological condition that can cause multiple symptoms including intense, debilitating headaches, nausea, vomiting, and extreme sensitivity to light and sound.
Migraine.causes: Hormonal changes, stress, sensory stimuli, sleep changes, physical factors, food additives, medications.
Migraine.symptoms: Acidity, indigestion, headache, blurred and distorted vision, excessive hunger, nausea, sensitivity to light.
Migraine.prevention: Identify and avoid triggers, maintain regular sleep, exercise regularly, manage stress, stay hydrated.

Cervical spondylosis.description: Cervical spondylosis is age-related wear affecting the spinal disks in your neck. As the disks dehydrate and shrink, signs of osteoarthritis develop.
Cervical spondylosis.causes: Aging, bone spurs, dehydrated spinal disks, herniated disks, injury, ligament stiffness, overuse.
Cervical spondylosis.symptoms: Back pain, weakness in limbs, neck pain, dizziness, loss of balance, grinding sensation, muscle spasms.
Cervical spondylosis.prevention: Maintain good posture, exercise neck muscles, avoid prolonged sitting, ergonomic workstation setup.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Brain hemorrhage is bleeding in or around the brain, which can be a type of stroke. It occurs when an artery bursts, causing localized bleeding in the surrounding tissues.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).causes: High blood pressure, aneurysm, arteriovenous malformation, blood clotting disorders, head injury.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).symptoms: Vomiting, headache, weakness in limbs, altered sensorium, stiff neck, sudden severe headache, vision problems.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).prevention: Control blood pressure, treat blood clotting disorders, avoid head injuries, manage risk factors for stroke.

Jaundice.description: Jaundice is a condition where the skin, whites of the eyes, and mucous membranes turn yellow due to high levels of bilirubin, a yellow-orange bile pigment.
Jaundice.causes: Liver disease, bile duct obstruction, hemolytic anemia, hepatitis, alcoholic liver disease, certain medications.
Jaundice.symptoms: Itching, vomiting, fatigue, weight loss, high fever, yellow skin, dark urine, pale stools.
Jaundice.prevention: Vaccination against hepatitis, avoid alcohol, maintain healthy weight, avoid risky behaviors that can lead to hepatitis.

Malaria.description: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites that are transmitted to people through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
Malaria.causes: Plasmodium parasite transmitted by infected Anopheles mosquitoes, rarely through blood transfusions or shared needles.
Malaria.symptoms: Chills, vomiting, high fever, sweating, headache, fatigue, nausea, muscle pain.
Malaria.prevention: Use mosquito repellents, sleep under mosquito nets, take antimalarial medications, wear protective clothing.

Chicken pox.description: Chickenpox is a highly contagious viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus, characterized by an itchy rash with small, fluid-filled blisters.
Chicken pox.causes: Varicella-zoster virus spread through direct contact with the rash, coughing, or sneezing.
Chicken pox.symptoms: Itching, skin rash, fatigue, lethargy, high fever, loss of appetite, headache, fluid-filled blisters.
Chicken pox.prevention: Vaccination, avoid contact with infected persons, maintain good hygiene, do not share personal items.

Dengue.description: Dengue fever is a mosquito-borne tropical disease caused by the dengue virus. Symptoms typically begin three to fourteen days after infection.
Dengue.causes: Dengue virus transmitted by the bite of Aedes mosquitoes, primarily Aedes aegypti.
Dengue.symptoms: Skin rash, chills, joint pain, vomiting, fatigue, high fever, severe headache, pain behind the eyes.
Dengue.prevention: Eliminate mosquito breeding sites, use mosquito repellents, wear protective clothing, use mosquito nets.

Typhoid.description: Typhoid fever is a bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi that can spread throughout the body, affecting many organs.
Typhoid.causes: Salmonella typhi bacteria spread through contaminated food or water, poor sanitation.
Typhoid.symptoms: Chills, vomiting, fatigue, high fever, headache, abdominal pain, rose-colored spots, constipation or diarrhea.
Typhoid.prevention: Vaccination, drink safe water, eat properly cooked food, practice good hand hygiene, avoid street food.

Hepatitis A.description: Hepatitis A is a highly contagious liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus. It ranges in severity from a mild illness to a severe one.
Hepatitis A.causes: Hepatitis A virus spread through contaminated food or water, close contact with infected persons.
Hepatitis A.symptoms: Joint pain, vomiting, yellowish skin, dark urine, nausea, fatigue, loss of appetite, abdominal pain.
Hepatitis A.prevention: Vaccination, wash hands thoroughly, avoid contaminated food and water, practice good hygiene.

Hepatitis B.description: Hepatitis B is a serious liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus (HBV). It can become a chronic condition leading to liver failure, liver cancer, or cirrhosis.
Hepatitis B.causes: HBV transmitted through infected blood, sexual contact, sharing needles, mother to newborn.
Hepatitis B.symptoms: Itching, fatigue, lethargy, yellowish skin, dark urine, abdominal pain, nausea, loss of appetite.
Hepatitis B.prevention: Vaccination, practice safe sex, avoid sharing needles, ensure blood products are tested.

Hepatitis C.description: Hepatitis C is a viral infection that causes liver inflammation, sometimes leading to serious liver damage. The hepatitis C virus spreads through contaminated blood.
Hepatitis C.causes: HCV transmitted through sharing needles, blood transfusions before 1992, sexual contact, mother to child.
Hepatitis C.symptoms: Fatigue, yellowish skin, nausea, loss of appetite, family history, bleeding easily, bruising easily.
Hepatitis C.prevention: Avoid sharing needles, ensure sterile medical equipment, practice safe sex, avoid sharing personal items.

Hepatitis D.description: Hepatitis D is a liver disease caused by the hepatitis D virus (HDV). It only infects people who are already infected with the hepatitis B virus.
Hepatitis D.causes: HDV transmitted through infected blood or sexual contact, only occurs in those with hepatitis B.
Hepatitis D.symptoms: Joint pain, vomiting, fatigue, yellowish skin, dark urine, abdominal pain, nausea.
Hepatitis D.prevention: Hepatitis B vaccination protects against HDV, avoid sharing needles, practice safe sex.

Hepatitis E.description: Hepatitis E is a liver disease caused by infection with a virus known as hepatitis E virus (HEV). It is transmitted through contaminated water.
Hepatitis E.causes: HEV spread through contaminated water and food, especially in areas with poor sanitation.
Hepatitis E.symptoms: Joint pain, vomiting, fatigue, high fever, yellowish skin, dark urine, loss of appetite.
Hepatitis E.prevention: Drink safe water, practice good food hygiene, avoid raw or undercooked food from potentially contaminated areas.

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Alcoholic hepatitis is an inflammation of the liver caused by drinking excessive amounts of alcohol over an extended period of time.
Alcoholic hepatitis.causes: Heavy, long-term alcohol consumption that damages liver cells and causes inflammation.
Alcoholic hepatitis.symptoms: Vomiting, yellowish skin, abdominal pain, swelling of stomach, history of alcohol consumption, fatigue, nausea.
Alcoholic hepatitis.prevention: Limit or avoid alcohol consumption, maintain healthy weight, get vaccinated for hepatitis, eat a balanced diet.

Tuberculosis.description: Tuberculosis (TB) is an infectious disease usually caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria that mainly affects the lungs.
Tuberculosis.causes: Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria spread through air when infected person coughs, speaks, or sneezes.
Tuberculosis.symptoms: Chills, vomiting, fatigue, weight loss, cough, night sweats, fever, blood in sputum.
Tuberculosis.prevention: BCG vaccination, proper ventilation, regular testing for high-risk groups, complete treatment course.

Common Cold.description: The common cold is a viral infection of the upper respiratory tract. The most common cause is rhinovirus, but many different viruses can cause a cold.
Common Cold.causes: Rhinovirus and other viruses spread through droplets from coughs, sneezes, or hand-to-hand contact.
Common Cold.symptoms: Continuous sneezing, chills, fatigue, cough, high fever, runny nose, sore throat, body aches.
Common Cold.prevention: Wash hands frequently, avoid close contact with sick people, avoid touching face, boost immune system.

Pneumonia.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
Pneumonia.causes: Bacteria (most commonly Streptococcus pneumoniae), viruses, or fungi. Can also result from aspiration.
Pneumonia.symptoms: Chills, fatigue, cough, high fever, breathlessness, chest pain, nausea, vomiting.
Pneumonia.prevention: Vaccination, good hygiene, avoid smoking, strengthen immune system, treat respiratory infections promptly.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).description: Hemorrhoids are swollen veins in the lower rectum and anus. They are very common, especially during pregnancy and after childbirth.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).causes: Straining during bowel movements, chronic constipation or diarrhea, low-fiber diet, pregnancy, obesity.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).symptoms: Constipation, pain during bowel movements, pain in anal region, bloody stool, irritation in anus, swelling.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).prevention: High-fiber diet, stay hydrated, avoid straining, exercise regularly, avoid prolonged sitting on toilet.

Heart attack.description: A heart attack occurs when blood flow to part of the heart muscle is blocked, usually by a blood clot. This can permanently damage or destroy heart muscle.
Heart attack.causes: Coronary artery disease, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, smoking, diabetes, obesity.
Heart attack.symptoms: Vomiting, breathlessness, sweating, chest pain, fatigue, arm pain, jaw pain, light-headedness.
Heart attack.prevention: Healthy diet, regular exercise, quit smoking, control blood pressure and cholesterol, manage diabetes.

Varicose veins.description: Varicose veins are enlarged, swollen, and twisting veins, often appearing blue or dark purple. They happen when faulty valves in the veins allow blood to flow in the wrong direction.
Varicose veins.causes: Age, pregnancy, family history, standing or sitting for long periods, obesity.
Varicose veins.symptoms: Fatigue, cramps, bruising, obesity, swollen legs, aching pain in legs, muscle cramping, swelling.
Varicose veins.prevention: Exercise regularly, maintain healthy weight, avoid prolonged standing, elevate legs, wear compression stockings.

Hypothyroidism.description: Hypothyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone. It can cause a number of symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and feeling cold.
Hypothyroidism.causes: Hashimoto's disease, thyroid surgery, radiation therapy, medications, pregnancy, iodine deficiency.
Hypothyroidism.symptoms: Fatigue, weight gain, cold hands and feet, constipation, mood swings, puffy face, muscle weakness.
Hypothyroidism.prevention: Adequate iodine intake, regular thyroid function tests, avoid excessive goitrogen consumption.

Hyperthyroidism.description: Hyperthyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland produces more thyroid hormone than the body needs, speeding up many bodily functions.
Hyperthyroidism.causes: Graves' disease, thyroid nodules, thyroiditis, excessive iodine intake, too much thyroid medication.
Hyperthyroidism.symptoms: Fatigue, mood swings, weight loss, restlessness, sweating, rapid heartbeat, tremors, heat sensitivity.
Hyperthyroidism.prevention: Regular thyroid check-ups, manage stress, adequate iodine intake but not excessive.

Hypoglycemia.description: Hypoglycemia is a condition where blood sugar (glucose) level drops below the normal range, causing symptoms like shakiness, anxiety, and difficulty concentrating.
Hypoglycemia.causes: Diabetes medications, excessive alcohol, critical illness, hormonal deficiencies, skipping meals.
Hypoglycemia.symptoms: Fatigue, restlessness, sweating, excessive hunger, dizziness, shakiness, confusion, irritability.
Hypoglycemia.prevention: Regular meal schedules, monitor blood glucose levels, adjust medications with doctor supervision, carry glucose tablets.

Osteoarthritis.description: Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, affecting millions of people worldwide. It occurs when the protective cartilage on the ends of bones wears down over time.
Osteoarthritis.causes: Age, joint injury, obesity, genetics, bone deformities, repetitive stress on joints.
Osteoarthritis.symptoms: Joint pain, neck pain, knee pain, hip joint pain, swelling joints, stiffness, reduced range of motion.
Osteoarthritis.prevention: Maintain healthy weight, exercise regularly, protect joints from injury, control blood sugar levels.

Arthritis.description: Arthritis is inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain and stiffness that can worsen with age. The most common types are osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.
Arthritis.causes: Family history, age, previous joint injury, obesity, certain autoimmune conditions.
Arthritis.symptoms: Muscle weakness, stiff neck, swelling joints, movement stiffness, painful walking, fatigue, reduced range of motion.
Arthritis.prevention: Exercise regularly, maintain healthy weight, protect joints, eat omega-3 rich diet, avoid smoking.

(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.description: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo is characterized by brief episodes of mild to intense dizziness. It is usually triggered by specific changes in head position.
(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.causes: Displacement of calcium crystals in inner ear, head injury, inner ear disorders, prolonged bed rest.
(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.symptoms: Vomiting, headache, nausea, spinning movements, loss of balance, dizziness, unsteady feeling.
(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.prevention: Avoid sudden head movements, sleep with head slightly elevated, canalith repositioning procedures.

Acne.description: Acne vulgaris is a chronic skin disease involving blockage and inflammation of pilosebaceous units. It is the most common skin disorder in the world.
Acne.causes: Excess oil production, bacteria, hormonal changes, certain medications, diet, stress.
Acne.symptoms: Skin rash, pus-filled pimples, blackheads, scarring, skin peeling, whiteheads, oily skin.
Acne.prevention: Regular face washing, avoid heavy cosmetics, eat healthy diet, avoid touching face, manage stress.

Urinary tract infection.description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra. Most infections involve the lower urinary tract.
Urinary tract infection.causes: Bacteria, usually E. coli, entering the urinary tract. Risk factors include female anatomy, sexual activity, certain contraceptives.
Urinary tract infection.symptoms: Burning micturition, bladder discomfort, foul smell of urine, continuous feel of urine, fatigue, cloudy urine.
Urinary tract infection.prevention: Stay hydrated, urinate after intercourse, wipe front to back, avoid irritating products, empty bladder regularly.

Psoriasis.description: Psoriasis is a skin disease that causes red, itchy scaly patches, most commonly on the knees, elbows, trunk, and scalp. It is a chronic long-lasting disease.
Psoriasis.causes: Immune system triggers, genetics, infections, injuries to skin, smoking, alcohol, stress.
Psoriasis.symptoms: Skin rash, joint pain, skin peeling, silver-like dusting, small dents in nails, dry cracked skin.
Psoriasis.prevention: Avoid triggers, moisturize skin, manage stress, avoid smoking and alcohol, protect skin from injury.

Impetigo.description: Impetigo is a common and highly contagious skin infection that mainly affects infants and children. It appears as reddish sores on the face.
Impetigo.causes: Staphylococcus aureus or Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria entering through skin breaks.
Impetigo.symptoms: Skin rash, high fever, blister, red sore around nose, yellow crust ooze, itching, swollen lymph nodes.
Impetigo.prevention: Good hand hygiene, keep wounds clean, avoid sharing personal items, treat cuts and scrapes promptly.
